knownFacts: gateway process: node.exe running openclaw.mjs gateway
active installed plugin path was promoted
currentBlockers: The remaining blocker is not Envoy source code, installed plugin files, or gateway availability. The remaining blocker is absence of a proven supported noninteractive OpenClaw protocol for listing and invoking loaded plugin tools.
maintainerQuestions: What is the supported noninteractive command or RPC method to list loaded plugin tools?
What is the supported noninteractive command or RPC method to invoke a loaded plugin tool?
Does openclaw gateway call support plugin tool invocation? If yes, what method name and payload shape?
Is there an internal JSON-RPC method equivalent to plugin.tools.list, plugin.tools.inspect, or plugin.tools.call?
Where in source are gateway RPC methods registered?
Where in source are plugin manifests loaded into runtime state?
Where in source are plugin tools exposed to the model/UI?
Are plugin tools callable outside the model loop?
Is there a read-only tool metadata flag or safety classification already supported?
What is the intended way to verify a plugin tool is callable after local install?
requestedSourceModules: CLI command definitions for plugins/gateway call
safeAcceptanceCriteria: enough source mapping to patch safely if no protocol exists
